articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Landlord Alarm:** 82% of landlords express concern over the Renters’ Rights Act, with 44% seriously worried about its impact.  
- **Portfolio Pessimism:** 75% feel regulations deter portfolio growth, and 49% plan to sell or downsize within the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants, with a looming 2030 mandate for a minimum C rating.
- Increasing tenant demand for energy efficiency is driven by rising energy costs due to global factors, making it a key consideration in rental decisions.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Under 25% of UK landlords believe planning reform will benefit their operations, indicating a significant lack of confidence in the proposed changes.  
- Landbay's findings suggest that the anticipated improvements in planning policy are falling flat in the eyes of property investors.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- **External Vigilance Required**: Landlords are urged to monitor outdoor pipework, as over 58% of hidden leaks start outside properties.  
- **Potentially Costly Consequences**: Ignoring external leaks can lead to significant repair costs, making maintenance crucial for landlords.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective from May 1, 2026, will introduce significant changes to the Private Rented Sector, enhancing tenant protections while maintaining landlord rights.  
- Landlords are advised to prepare for new regulations and oversight, ensuring compliance to avoid potential pitfalls as the deadline approaches.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- 84% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act set to take effect on May 1.  
- 58% of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance with the new legislation.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting strategies from expansion to restructuring portfolios and financing due to changing rental demand.
- The Office for National Statistics (ONS) highlights a slowdown in rental growth, prompting landlords to prioritize portfolio optimization over expansion.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals that landlords and investors are significantly driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.
- The increasing competition from property investors is creating barriers for those looking to enter homeownership.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the upcoming government to acknowledge the significance of the private rented sector (PRS) with a formal strategy.  
- A dedicated approach is needed to curb the exodus of landlords from Scotland's housing market.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams must register as tax advisors with HMRC to avoid hefty penalties.  
- Non-compliance could result in fines of up to £10,000, placing additional pressure on landlords to ensure their advisors are properly registered.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that implementing licensing fees for landlords, set at £694 per property, won’t result in increased rent prices for tenants.
- Landlords are concerned about the financial burden of the licensing costs, which they will not be able to recover.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook stated that the party will not implement rent controls.  
- The declaration emphasizes Labour's commitment to market-driven rental policies amid ongoing housing debates.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to impose stricter regulations to curb the "HMO landlord free for all" in the UK.  
- Detailed guidelines for Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) are in development to ensure better management and standards.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicks off on Monday to educate tenants about their rights under the Renters Rights Act.
- Initiatives aim to empower renters by providing essential knowledge and tools to navigate their rights effectively.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The new Renters Rights Act has led to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, confirming predictions made by property expert Paul Shamplina.  
- Landlords are reacting quickly to the changes, highlighting ongoing tensions in the rental market amidst evolving legislation.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing as landlords adjust ahead of the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to new data from Hamptons.  
- The rise in rental prices highlights a potential market shift as regulations change in the UK.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to become law in just 10 days, is expected to significantly impact the hobbyist landlord market.  
- Critics argue that the new regulations will deter casual investors from renting out properties, potentially leading to a decrease in affordable rental options.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly embracing property investment as a viable business opportunity, despite facing regulatory challenges and higher taxes.
- This trend highlights a shift in investment strategies among younger generations, showcasing their resilience and adaptability in navigating the evolving property landscape.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers face increasing pressure as many are slashing asking prices, experiencing failed sales, or pulling listings entirely, according to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson.  
- The current market conditions present significant challenges for property sellers, highlighting a turbulent landscape in the UK real estate sector.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is on the rise, it has been revealed.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC intensifies oversight of property valuations, leading to a significant increase in cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).
- Experts warn that heightened scrutiny could impact property transactions and taxation rates.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A property listing in Penrith features an unexpected surprise: a sleeping tenant captured in the photos and video of the £135k terrace home.  
- The unintentional inclusion raises eyebrows and highlights potential issues in handling tenant sales amid ongoing housing market concerns.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Conveyancing Task Force labels referral fees as a “hidden tax” that increases costs for homebuyers.  
- These fees act as an "unseen surcharge," adding financial burden to the property buying process.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is expanding its operations into Scotland by partnering with Clyde Property Group to manage its West of Scotland portfolio.  
- David Alexander from Lomond highlights the strategic move to strengthen their presence in the northern market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons reports a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue, attributed to unexpected geopolitical challenges impacting buyer engagement.  
- CEO Guy Gittins highlights lower-than-anticipated new buyer activity as a key factor behind the significant decline.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation in the UK is climbing, largely driven by rising fuel and transport costs due to the Middle East conflict.  
- Experts warn that these price increases are likely to ripple through the broader economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of newly built homes are never listed on the open sales market, highlighting a significant disconnect in the housing market.
- Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, emphasizes the pressing need for increased transparency and access in home sales.
